facts_disclosed: Zara moved out, Priya (junior doctor) moved in

turns.content: I need advice on living with someone you literally never see. like is that normal for flatmates or is it weird

It's more common than you'd think! Plenty of flatmates basically coexist without overlapping much, especially if you're both working long or irregular hours. Is this a new living situation, or has it always been like this?

new yeah. so my old flatmate Zara moved in with her boyfriend and I had to find someone pretty quickly cos I can't cover the full rent on my own. Priya moved in about 3 weeks ago. she's genuinely really nice but she works at Manchester Royal Infirmary — she's a junior doctor — so her schedule is absolutely mental

Junior doctors at Manchester Royal Infirmary are on some of the most brutal rotations going — 12-hour shifts, nights, weekends. So it's not surprising you barely cross paths. Three weeks is also still very early — you're both still figuring out each other's rhythms.

What does a typical week look like for you both? Are you on completely opposite schedules?

pretty much. I work normal office hours, like 8 to 6ish sometimes later. she does these blocks of nights and then days and it changes every few weeks. sometimes I come home and she's asleep, sometimes she's already left. we've probably had like 3 proper conversations since she moved in loool

Three proper conversations in three weeks with those schedules is honestly about right. It's not weird — it's just the reality of two people with demanding jobs living under the same roof.

The question is: does it bother you? Some people love a flatmate who's basically a ghost — you get the rent split without the social obligation. Other people need some level of connection to feel like it's a home rather than just a housing arrangement. Which camp are you in?

tbh I think I'm somewhere in the middle. I don't need us to be best mates but it would be nice to at least like know each other a bit? with Zara we were proper close, we'd cook together and watch stuff in the evenings. obviously that was different cos we'd been friends since uni. Priya I found through spareroom so we're basically strangers

That's a big adjustment — going from living with a close friend to someone you found online. It's natural to feel the gap, especially when you had that easy domestic companionship with Zara. But it doesn't mean things with Priya can't develop, it just takes more intentional effort when the schedules don't create natural hangout time.

A few low-effort things that help:

1. **A WhatsApp thread** between you two for house stuff — but also just to chat casually. Easier than catching each other in person. "Hey how was your shift" goes a long way.
2. **One planned meal together** when your schedules align — even if it's once a fortnight. Doesn't have to be fancy, just sitting down together for 30 minutes.
3. **Leave notes or texts** about small things — "made extra pasta, it's in the fridge if you want" kind of stuff. Builds goodwill without requiring face time.

Does Priya seem like she'd be up for that, or is she more of a keep-to-herself person?

no she's actually really friendly when we do see each other. like last Saturday we were both home at the same time and she made chai and we sat and talked for ages. she's from Leicester, her family are Hindu, she told me about her rotation and honestly junior doctor life sounds horrifying. 90 hour weeks sometimes

90 hours is genuinely brutal — and that's a shared experience you two have more than you might think. Long hours, high pressure, being young and navigating a demanding career. Different fields but similar energy. The fact that she made chai and you talked for ages when you did overlap is a really good sign — it means the connection is there, you just need more opportunities for it.

Saturday catch-ups might be your window. Even if it's not every week, having that as a rough ritual when you're both around could be the thing that turns her from "nice flatmate" into someone you actually know.

yeah that's true. my hours in advisory are pretty long too so I can relate. also one thing — she's really quiet when she comes in late which I massively appreciate. Zara used to come home from nights out and wake me up banging around the kitchen loool. Priya is like a ninja

Ha — the ninja flatmate is a genuine blessing, especially when you need your sleep. Junior doctors learn to be quiet out of necessity because they live around other people on opposite shift patterns. That's a huge point in Priya's favour.

Sounds like you've actually landed a good flatmate situation, even if it doesn't look like what you had with Zara. Different dynamic but different isn't bad — quiet, respectful, friendly when you do cross paths, and not a financial stress. Give it a couple more months and you'll have a better feel for the rhythm.

yeah you're right. I think I was just comparing it to living with Zara and feeling like something was missing. but honestly it's fine. Priya's great, we're both just busy people. I should probably text her more instead of waiting to bump into her in the kitchen

Exactly — text the way you'd interact if you had time in person. Send her random stuff, ask how her shift was, share a meme. That's how you build the connection around the schedule gap. It'll feel more like living with someone and less like time-sharing a flat.

And hey, missing Zara is allowed too. You can appreciate what you have with Priya and still miss what you had before.

ugh yeah I do miss Zara tbh. happy for her obviously, her boyfriend's really nice, but the flat feels different without her. anyway thanks for this. I'm going to text Priya now and ask if she wants to do dinner this weekend
That's a great shout — dinner together this weekend sounds like exactly the right move. Hope it goes well! And give yourself time to adjust, three weeks is nothing.
